Best for: Eco-conscious owners wanting full transparency. Check price on Amazon →

7. PureBites Chicken Freeze-Dried Topper — Best Limited Ingredient

For cats with allergies or sensitive stomachs, the PureBites Chicken Freeze-Dried Raw Topper offers a true limited-ingredient solution. With just one ingredient — 100% pure USA-sourced chicken breast — it eliminates the risk of triggering food sensitivities while delivering a topper most cats adore.

Key features: Single ingredient: freeze-dried raw chicken breast. No additives, preservatives, or fillers. Made in the USA with humanely raised chicken. Freeze-dried to lock in natural flavor and nutrients. Soft, chewy texture rehydrates quickly.

What we love: For cats with food allergies, this is the safest option to try. The ingredient list doesn't get any simpler. PureBites also offers a freeze-dried salmon variety for cats who prefer fish.

ProsCons
Single-ingredient, hypoallergenicOnly chicken variety available
100% USA-sourced chickenPricier than multi-ingredient options
No additives or preservativesSome cats may prefer mixed flavors
Great for allergy-prone cats—

Best for: Cats with food sensitivities. Check price on Amazon →

8. Fancy Feast Natural Broths — Best Budget Broth Topper

If you're looking for an affordable broth-based topper, the Fancy Feast Natural Cat Food Topper Broths deliver excellent value. These single-serving broth cups are made with real fish or chicken in a savory broth.

Key features: Real protein as the first ingredient. Available in tuna, salmon, and chicken. Grain-free and carrageenan-free. Convenient peel-and-serve cups with no preparation needed.

What we love: The price per serving is hard to beat. The peel-and-serve cups couldn't be more convenient. The tuna recipe provides excellent omega-3 content. A great everyday hydration option.

ProsCons
Very affordableLess protein-dense
Convenient peel-and-serveIndividual cups create waste
Grain-free, no artificial ingredientsBroth texture only
Great hydration boost—

Best for: Budget-conscious owners wanting convenience. Check price on Amazon →

Comparison Table

TopperTypeProtein SourceKey BenefitPrice
Stella & Chewy'sFreeze-dried rawChicken, duck, rabbit, salmonHighest protein, most versatile$$
Tiki Cat VelvetWet mousseChicken, tuna, salmonSmooth texture$
Honest Kitchen BrothBone brothChickenHydration, joint support$
Vital EssentialsFreeze-dried whole preyWhole minnowsSingle ingredient, omega-3s$
Purina FortiFloraProbiotic powderAnimal digest baseDual probiotic + flavor$
Open Farm Mix-InsFreeze-dried rawBeef, chicken, salmonEthical sourcing$$$
PureBitesFreeze-dried rawChicken breastLimited ingredient, hypoallergenic$$
Fancy Feast BrothsBroth cupTuna, salmon, chickenBudget hydration$

How to Use Cat Food Toppers Effectively

  1. Start small: Begin with a pinch of freeze-dried or a teaspoon of broth.
  2. Introduce gradually: Mix thoroughly into food so they can't separate it.
  3. Rotate flavors: Alternate between 2-3 toppers to maintain interest.
  4. Adjust portions: Toppers add calories — reduce main food slightly if needed.
  5. Watch for sensitivities: Introduce one at a time and monitor for reactions.

Frequently Asked Questions

Can I use toppers with every meal?

Yes, most are safe for daily use. Vary the type for nutritional variety.

Are toppers complete nutrition?

Most are supplements, not complete meals. Use alongside nutritionally complete cat food.

How much topper should I use?

Generally 1-2 tablespoons or one packet per meal. Adjust based on your cat's size.

Can I use human food as a topper?

Cooked, unseasoned chicken or canned pumpkin can work, but commercial toppers provide balanced nutrition.

Final Verdict

The Stella & Chewy's Meal Mixers earn our top recommendation. For hydration, the Honest Kitchen Bone Broth is a must-have. For digestive sensitivity, Purina FortiFlora delivers dual benefits. Every cat has unique preferences — experiment with different types and flavors to find the perfect match.

title: 5 Best Rat Cages and Accessories in 2026: Spacious Multi-Level Homes
description: Rats need spacious, multi-level homes with plenty of enrichment. Our 2026 guide covers the 5 best rat cages and essential accessories for happy, healthy pet rats.
content:
Pet rats are intelligent, social, and endlessly entertaining companions, but they have very specific housing requirements. A proper rat cage isn't just a place for them to sleep — it's their entire world. Rats need vertical space for climbing, solid flooring to prevent foot injuries, good ventilation, and enough room to accommodate multiple rats (since they should never be kept alone).

The best rat cages feature multiple levels, wide doors for easy cleaning, deep base pans to contain bedding, and bar spacing narrow enough to prevent escapes. They also need to accommodate accessories like hammocks, ropes, wheels, and hiding spots that make a rat's life truly enriching.

In this guide, we've reviewed the 5 best rat cages and essential accessories available in 2026, from budget-friendly starter cages to premium multi-level condos. We've also included essential accessories that every rat owner should consider.

What to Look for in a Rat Cage

  • Size: Minimum 2.5 cubic feet per rat. A cage of at least 24" × 18" × 24" is a good starting point for a pair.
  • Bar spacing: No more than 1/2 inch for adults, 1/4 inch for babies. Rats squeeze through surprisingly small gaps.
  • Ventilation: Wire or mesh sides are essential. Glass aquariums trap ammonia fumes that cause respiratory problems.
  • Solid floors: At least some solid platforms are necessary. Wire floors cause bumblefoot (painful foot infections).
  • Accessibility: Large doors or removable tops make weekly deep cleaning much easier.
  • Vertical space: Rats are natural climbers. Multiple levels connected by ramps, ladders, or ropes keep them active.

How We Tested and Selected

Our team evaluated 12 rat cages over several months, considering assembly difficulty, cleaning ease, durability, safety features, and value. We consulted experienced rat owners, breeders, and veterinarians.

1. Critter Nation Rat Cage — Best Overall Multi-Level Cage

The Critter Nation Rat Cage is widely considered the gold standard in the rat community. This single-unit cage provides 23 cubic feet of living space with four levels connected by wide ramps. The design prioritizes both rat comfort and owner convenience.

Key features: 1/2-inch bar spacing (safe for most adult males), full-width double doors that swing 180 degrees open, a deep 4-inch base pan, and four sturdy wire shelves. Powder-coated wire is durable and easy to clean. Casters allow easy movement.

What we love: The full-width doors make cleaning, rearranging, and interacting a breeze. The deep pan contains even enthusiastic diggers. Textured ramps prevent slips. This cage is expandable — stack a second unit on top for even more space.

ProsCons
Spacious 23 cu ft with 4 levelsHigher price point
Full-width double doorsHeavy and bulky
Deep 4-inch base panWire shelves need covering
Expandable with add-on unitsAssembly takes time

Best for: Owners wanting the best possible home for their rats. Check price on Amazon →

2. Prevue Pet Products Rat Cage — Best Value Mid-Size

The Prevue Pet Products Rat Cage offers 18.5 cubic feet with three levels. One of the most popular mid-range cages, it's ideal for a pair or small group of rats. Pair it with a Prevue small animal exercise wheel for great enrichment.

Key features: 3/8-inch bar spacing (very secure for smaller breeds), three levels with ramps, deep plastic base pan, two large front doors. Powder-coated wire with non-toxic finish.

What we love: Hard-to-beat value. 3/8-inch spacing provides excellent security for babies. Ramps have cross-hatch textures for good traction. Relatively easy to assemble.

ProsCons
Great value for the sizeBase pan could be deeper
3/8-inch bar spacingDoors could be wider
Three levels with good vertical spaceShelves need covering

Best for: Owners with 2-3 rats seeking quality without premium pricing. Check price on Amazon →

3. Kaytee Rat Home — Best Budget Starter Cage

The Kaytee Rat Home is an affordable option for new rat owners. Two levels, a deep base pan, 1/2-inch bar spacing, and a top carrying handle. For fleece liners, check the Kaytee fleece cage liners designed to fit this cage.

Key features: Powder-coated wire, removable plastic base, included water bottle and food dish. Lightweight and fits on a desk or dresser.

What we love: For the price, a solid introduction to rat keeping. Lightweight, portable. Top handle makes transport convenient. Includes starter accessories.

ProsCons
Very affordableSmaller than ideal for groups
Lightweight and portableOnly 2 levels
Includes starter accessoriesPlastic ramp can be slippery

Best for: New rat owners, young rats, or as a hospital cage. Check price on Amazon →

4. Ferret Nation Cage — Best Expandable System (Modified)

Experienced rat owners often choose the Ferret Nation Cage and modify it for rats. While designed for ferrets, it shares features with Critter Nation with wider bar spacing — better for larger breeds but needing mesh modification for small rats.

Key features: 1-inch bar spacing (requires mesh modification for smaller rats), three full shelves, full-width double doors, deep base pan. 32"×20"×36". Expandable by stacking additional units.

What we love: Fantastic floor space for active rats. Full-width doors make cleaning easy. Modular design means you can start with one and expand. Many owners end up with a double stack.

ProsCons
Excellent vertical spaceBar spacing needs modification
Expandable modular designMore expensive
Full-width doorsBulky and heavy

Best for: Owners with 3+ rats wanting modular expansion. Check price on Amazon →

5. Rat Accessories Bundle — Best Cage Add-Ons

The Rat Starter Kit Accessories Bundle includes hammocks, hideouts, chew toys, and fleece cage liners — everything to outfit your cage without buying 7-8 separate items. For additional enrichment, the rat chew toy variety pack keeps teeth healthy.

Key features: Two fleece hammocks, a hideout hut, lava ledge for nail trimming, various chew sticks and wooden toys, fleece cage liners. Machine-washable hammocks with metal clips.

What we love: The convenience factor is enormous. Hammocks are well-made with reinforced stitching. Lava ledges keep nails naturally trimmed. The fleece liners are a great alternative to loose bedding for rats with respiratory sensitivity.

ProsCons
Comprehensive starter setNot all items may suit your cage
Machine-washable hammocksSome items may be smaller than expected
Nail-trimming lava ledgesChew toys may not last with heavy chewers

Best for: New rat owners needing essential enrichment. Check price on Amazon →

Essential Rat Cage Accessories

  • Hammocks: Rats adore fleece hammocks for sleeping and nesting. Machine washable.
  • Solid shelf covers: Cover wire shelves with fleece or liners to prevent bumblefoot.
  • Lava ledges: Pumice ledges provide resting platforms and naturally file nails.
  • Water bottles: Constant fresh water access. 16-32 oz for 2-3 rats.
  • Hideouts: Multiple hideouts (cardboard, plastic igloos, wooden huts) prevent territorial disputes.
  • Chew toys: Rats' teeth never stop growing. Wood, pumice, and hard plastic keep teeth healthy.
  • Digging box: A shallow container with coconut fiber or fleece strips provides enrichment.

Cage Setup Guide

  1. Solid flooring: Cover wire shelves with fleece or liners.
  2. Vertical layers: Arrange shelves, hammocks, and ledges at different heights.
  3. Distribute resources: Place food, water, and hideouts on different levels.
  4. Add enrichment: Rotate toys, tunnels, and foraging opportunities regularly.
  5. Litter area: Rats can be litter trained! Place a pan with different substrate in one corner.
  6. Temperature: Keep cage away from drafts, direct sun, and extremes.

Frequently Asked Questions

How many rats should I keep together?

A minimum of two rats is essential. Groups of 3-4 are even better. Same-sex pairs or groups unless breeding.

How often should I clean the cage?

Spot-clean daily. Full bedding change every 5-7 days. Deep clean every 2-4 weeks.

Can I use a glass aquarium?

No. Poor ventilation leads to ammonia buildup and respiratory infections — the #1 health problem in rats.

What bedding is safest?

Paper-based bedding (Carefresh, Kaytee Clean & Cozy), aspen shavings, or kiln-dried pine pellets. Avoid cedar and aromatic pine.

Do rats need a wheel?

Yes, but a large solid-surface wheel (12+ inches). Wire or small wheels cause injuries.

Final Verdict

The Critter Nation Rat Cage is the ultimate choice for most owners. For budget-conscious buyers, the Prevue Pet Products Rat Cage offers excellent value. Don't forget enrichment accessories from the Rat Starter Accessories Bundle to keep your rats happy and engaged.
